theorems.statement: Mutation memory is monotone nondecreasing

theorems.name: Book6.mutationMemory_nonneg
theorems.statement: Mutation memory is always nonnegative

theorems.name: Book6.entropyGrowth_accum
theorems.statement: Telescoped n-step growth bound for an entropy sequence increasing by a fixed amount per step

theorems.name: Book6.entropyGrowth_unbounded
theorems.statement: A sequence growing by a fixed positive amount every step is unbounded above

theorems.name: Book6.regulatoryCycle_energy_bound
theorems.statement: Per-step free-energy bound telescopes to an n-step bound via the triangle inequality

theorems.name: Book6.configDim_mono
theorems.statement: Configuration-space dimension floor(lambda)+d0 is monotone nondecreasing in lambda

theorems.name: Book6.configDim_ge
theorems.statement: Configuration-space dimension is always at least the base dimension d0

theorems.name: Book6.identityCarrier_le_self
theorems.statement: The locality/exponential-decay bound forces Psi(x,y) <= Psi(x,x)

theorems.name: Book6.temperature_pos_iff
theorems.statement: Symbolic temperature (dS/dE)^-1 is positive iff dS/dE is positive

theorems.name: Book6.freeEnergy_antitone_in_entropy
theorems.statement: At fixed energy and positive temperature, free energy E-T*S strictly decreases as entropy strictly increases

theorems.name: Book6.transformationFamily_triple
theorems.statement: Two-step transformation composition collapses to one application at the combined parameter

theorems.name: Book6.transformationFamily_bracketing_agrees
theorems.statement: If the parameter operation is associative, both bracketings of a three-fold composition agree

theorems.name: Book6.bifurcation_offset_pos
theorems.statement: The bifurcation branching offset 2*(gammaMin-Upsilon)/lambdaBif is positive whenever Upsilon < gammaMin

theorems.name: Book6.no_retraction_of_not_injective
theorems.statement: A non-injective map admits no left inverse (retraction), the honest kernel of symbolic time irreversibility

theorems.name: Book6.mutationBifurcationBridge_iff
theorems.statement: Mutation occurs iff bifurcation occurs, by transitivity of two bridge equivalences

theorems.name: Book6.regulatoryBasin_power_argmax_exists
theorems.statement: A regulatory basin's power functional attains a maximum over any nonempty finite index set

theorems.name: Book6.confidence_sigma_pos
theorems.statement: The confidence field exp(-beta*Hconf) is always strictly positive

theorems.name: Book6.closedPower_exchange_sum_zero
theorems.statement: Antisymmetric internal pairwise power exchanges have zero double sum

theorems.name: Book6.closedPower_total_conserved
theorems.statement: A finite closed system evolving by internal antisymmetric exchange conserves total pairwise power

theorems.name: Book6.totalPairPower_eq_metabolicRate
theorems.statement: Book 6 total pair power is definitionally Book 3 metabolic rate on a finite carrier

theorems.name: Book6.closedPower_homeostatic_step
theorems.statement: One closed Book 6 power-exchange step preserves the Book 3 homeostatic band

theorems.name: Book6.closedPower_homeostatic_all
theorems.statement: Initial Book 3 metabolic homeostasis persists through every finite Book 6 power-evolution stage

theorems.name: Book6.confidencePower_gaussian_bound
theorems.statement: Gaussian-local power remains within the same envelope after weighting by confidence in the unit interval

theorems.name: Book6.symbolicInformation_relabel_invariant
theorems.statement: Finite relative information is invariant under any bijective relabeling of the symbolic state space

theorems.name: Book7.dualityRecovers
theorems.statement: If U is obtained from P by an involutive map f, applying f to U recovers P.

theorems.name: Book7.involutive_pair_witness
theorems.statement: The Bool swap witnesses R_n(id) != id but R_2n(id) = id: a single application moves every point, double application is the identity.

theorems.name: Book7.dz_zero_of_le
theorems.statement: The deadzone function dz_tau(e) is zero whenever the residual is inside the resolution deadband, |e| <= tau.

theorems.name: Book7.dz_contraction_bound
theorems.statement: One deadband-controller step contracts a residual outside the deadband: |e'| <= (1-k)|e| + k*tau + W.

theorems.name: Book7.deadband_contraction
theorems.statement: Structure-level restatement of dz_contraction_bound for a DeadbandController's own residual sequence.

theorems.name: Book7.deadband_region_invariant
theorems.statement: Once the residual is within the ultimate bound tau + W/k, the next step stays within it (invariance).

theorems.name: Book7.deadband_strict_decrease
theorems.statement: Outside the ultimate-bound region, one controller step strictly shrinks the residual.

theorems.name: Book7.deadband_confidence_ascent
theorems.statement: The confidence S_n = exp(-lambda|e_n|) strictly increases while the residual is outside the ultimate-bound region.

theorems.name: Book7.deadband_geometric_decay
theorems.statement: In the undisturbed case W=0, the deadband gap |e_n|-tau decays geometrically at rate (1-k)^n.

theorems.name: Book7.selfCorrection_succeeds
theorems.statement: If gain stays >= kmin > 0 and disturbance stays <= Wmax, the ultimate bound tau+W/k is controlled uniformly by tau+Wmax/kmin.

theorems.name: Book7.selfCorrection_fails_as_gain_vanishes
theorems.statement: Countermodel: for any n, a positive gain k <= 1 exists making the ultimate bound tau+W/k exceed n, i.e. the bound is unbounded as k -> 0+.

theorems.name: Book7.selfCorrection_fails_as_disturbance_grows
theorems.statement: Countermodel: for any target M, a disturbance bound W exists making tau+W/k exceed M at any fixed gain k, i.e. the bound is unbounded as W -> infinity.

theorems.name: Book7.caristiDescent_sum_le_energy_drop
theorems.statement: Telescoping: the sum of the first n Caristi displacements is bounded by the free-energy drop freeEnergy(0) - freeEnergy(n).

theorems.name: Book7.caristiDescent_total_displacement_bound
theorems.statement: The total displacement up to any step is bounded by the total free energy available, freeEnergy(0) - Fmin.

theorems.name: Book7.geometric_gap_decay
theorems.statement: If a nonnegative gap sequence contracts geometrically at rate q, the gap after n steps is at most q^n times the initial gap.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_idempotent
theorems.statement: If R's image lands in Fix(reflect) and R agrees with the identity there, then R is idempotent -- derived, not posited.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_linear_image_kernel_split
theorems.statement: A linear Book 7 orbit-limit splits every vector into stable image and transverse kernel components.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_derivative_image_kernel_split
theorems.statement: A differentiable nonlinear orbit-limit induces the same tangent splitting at every fixed image point.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_completeJacobian
theorems.statement: Reflection and drift form the complete Jacobian at every differentiable Book 7 orbit-limit fixed point.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_fixedLocusVelocity_iff
theorems.statement: Book 7 fixed-locus curve velocities are exactly the vectors fixed by the orbit-limit derivative projection.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_transverse_contracts
theorems.statement: Below the unit perturbation margin, every nonzero transverse direction strictly contracts in one complete linearized Euler step.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_transverse_iterates_tendsto_zero
theorems.statement: An invariant transverse drift at a differentiable orbit-limit yields the projection law, a geometric iterate envelope, and convergence to zero.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_transverse_eigenvalue_stable
theorems.statement: Every real transverse eigenmode at a differentiable orbit-limit has eigenvalue strictly inside the unit disk below the perturbation margin.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_transverse_jacobian_eigenmode_stable
theorems.statement: Every real transverse Jacobian eigenmode at a differentiable orbit-limit has a negative margin and exponentially decays to zero.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_completeJacobian_semigroup
theorems.statement: A differentiable orbit-limit supplies the projection law and the full complete-Jacobian operator semigroup with its generator equation.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_semigroup_transverse_eigenmode_tendsto_zero
theorems.statement: The full Book 7 semigroup acts by scalar exponential on each stable transverse eigenvector and its orbit converges to zero.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_base_fixed_but_recorded
theorems.statement: A reflective orbit-limit is visibly fixed while positive trace production advances its full observer-state.

theorems.name: Book7.tendsto_refinement_to_orbitLimit
theorems.statement: Every trajectory of a Book 4 contraction refinement converges to the value selected by its canonical Book 7 orbit-limit operator.

theorems.name: Book7.orbitLimit_iterate_fixed_under_representation
theorems.statement: Scholium fixed-point transport carries Book 4 recursive stability of a Book 7 orbit limit across representations

theorems.name: Book7.product_contraction
theorems.statement: If two cross maps are Lipschitz with constants kA, kB, the induced product map is Lipschitz with constant max(kA,kB) on the sup-product pseudometric.

theorems.name: Book7.mutualLimit_fixed
theorems.statement: The Book 4 contraction limit of the mutual-reflection operator satisfies both reciprocal fixed-pair equations.

theorems.name: Book7.reciprocalPair_unique
theorems.statement: Every reciprocal pair equals the canonical mutual-reflection limit under the complete-metric contraction hypotheses.

theorems.name: Book7.tendsto_mutualRefinement
theorems.statement: Every joint mutual-reflection trajectory converges to the unique reciprocal pair.

theorems.name: Book7.contraction_step
theorems.statement: A Lipschitz map with constant kappa < 1 and a known fixed point shrinks distance to that fixed point by at least factor kappa in one step.

theorems.name: Book7.exponent_uniqueness
theorems.statement: A strictly antitone real function is injective, so at most one point maps to any given value (in particular, to 2).

theorems.name: Book7.l1_l2_comparison
theorems.statement: On R^2, the L^1 norm |a|+|b| and the L^2 norm sqrt(a^2+b^2) bound each other within a factor of sqrt 2.

theorems.name: Book7.square_strictly_convex_midpoint
theorems.statement: x |-> x^2 is strictly midpoint-convex: ((x+y)/2)^2 < (x^2+y^2)/2 for x != y.

theorems.name: Book4A.stability_lower_bound
theorems.statement: If stability >= 1-eps with eps < epsCrit < 1, stability exceeds 1-epsCrit and is positive.

theorems.name: Book4A.recursive_encoding_partial_sum_le_total
theorems.statement: For a nonnegative summable distortion sequence, every finite partial sum is bounded by the total sum.

theorems.name: Book4A.RecursiveEncoding.cauchySeq
theorems.statement: A recursive encoding hierarchy with summably bounded successive distortions is Cauchy.

theorems.name: Book4A.RecursiveEncoding.exists_limit_with_tail_bound
theorems.statement: In a complete metric symbolic space, a recursive encoding converges to a representation whose remaining distortion is bounded by the tail budget.

theorems.name: Book4A.RecursiveEncoding.exists_fixed_limit_with_tail_bound
theorems.statement: A summably distorted continuous recursive encoding converges to a refinement-fixed representation while retaining its tail distortion bound.

theorems.name: Book4A.operator_noncommutativity_witness
theorems.statement: There exist two functions on Bool whose compositions differ depending on order.

theorems.name: Book4A.selfReferenceIterate_succ
theorems.statement: The self-reference operator satisfies S_(n+1) = R composed with S_n, via Function.iterate.

theorems.name: Book4A.selfReference_fixed_point
theorems.statement: A fixed point of R is a fixed point of every iterate S_n.

theorems.name: Book4A.boundary_forms_dichotomy
theorems.statement: A differentiation boundary either forms (curvature divergence exceeds threshold) or it does not; exhaustive dichotomy.

theorems.name: Book4A.kappa_nonneg
theorems.statement: Symbolic curvature (as a kernel-weighted squared residual) is non-negative.

theorems.name: Book4A.kappa_scale
theorems.statement: Symbolic curvature scales by alpha^2 under scaling the symbolic argument by alpha, given the reflexive operator acts linearly.

theorems.name: Book4A.kappa_reflexive_vanishing
theorems.statement: Symbolic curvature vanishes at a fixed point of the reflexive operator.

theorems.name: Book4A.kappa_observer_dependent
theorems.statement: Witness: two different kernel-energy constants assign different curvature to the same residual (observer dependence is real, not vacuous).

theorems.name: Book4A.transitionRate_pos
theorems.statement: The Arrhenius-form symbolic transition rate is strictly positive whenever its structural prefactor is.

theorems.name: Book4A.autoEncoderPattern_pos
theorems.statement: The auto-encoder's core symbolic pattern formula exp(-lambda*d) is always strictly positive.

theorems.name: Book4A.timescaleSeparation_micro_lt_observation
theorems.statement: Chained strict timescale separation is transitive end to end.

theorems.name: Book4A.autoEncoder_finite_sum_le
theorems.statement: Over a finite sample, total reconstruction error is bounded by sample size times the per-point error bound.

theorems.name: Book4A.SymbolicAutoEncoder.exact_reconstruction_of_eps_eq_zero
theorems.statement: A zero reconstruction budget forces the decoder to recover every encoded identity exactly.

theorems.name: Book4A.SymbolicAutoEncoder.identityPattern_eq_one_iff
theorems.statement: At nonzero sensitivity, auto-encoder identity-pattern weight is one exactly for exact reconstruction.

theorems.name: Book4A.SymbolicAutoEncoder.encode_injective_of_eps_eq_zero
theorems.statement: An exactly reconstructing symbolic auto-encoder has an injective encoding map.

theorems.name: Book4A.complexSymbolicDistance_re
theorems.statement: The real part of the observer-relative complex symbolic distance is the real displacement d_Re.

theorems.name: Book4A.complexSymbolicDistance_im
theorems.statement: The imaginary part of the observer-relative complex symbolic distance is the imaginary displacement d_Im.

theorems.name: Book4A.complexSymbolicDistance_re_le_abs
theorems.statement: The real displacement never exceeds the norm of the full complex symbolic distance.

theorems.name: Book4A.reintegrableIdentity_iff
theorems.statement: Identity is imaginatively reintegrable exactly when both real and imaginary displacements lie below their observer thresholds.

theorems.name: Book4A.ReintegrableIdentity.mono_thresholds
theorems.statement: Imaginative reintegration persists when either observer tolerance is enlarged.

theorems.name: Book4A.not_reintegrableIdentity_iff
theorems.statement: Imaginative reintegration fails exactly when the real or imaginary displacement breaches its threshold.

theorems.name: Book4A.uncanny_recognition_countermodel
theorems.statement: Witness where real displacement is within tolerance but imaginary displacement is not, so reintegrable identity genuinely fails (the uncanny-recognition case).

theorems.name: Book4A.quadrant_exhaustive
theorems.statement: Every point off both axes of (Re, Im) falls into exactly one of the four Event Horizon sign-quadrants.

theorems.name: Book4A.quadrants_disjoint
theorems.statement: Diametrically opposite Event Horizon quadrants (Pos-Pos and Neg-Neg) are mutually exclusive.

theorems.name: Book4A.spiralMagnitude_recurrence
theorems.statement: The spiral orbit magnitude obeys the per-step law r_(n+1) = rho * r_n.

theorems.name: Book4A.spiralPhase_recurrence
theorems.statement: The spiral orbit phase obeys the per-step law theta_(n+1) = theta_n + alpha.

theorems.name: Book4A.goldenRatio_sq
theorems.statement: The golden ratio satisfies phi^2 = phi + 1.

theorems.name: Book4A.goldenSpiral_recurrence
theorems.statement: The golden-ratio spiral magnitudes obey the balanced two-step (Fibonacci-type) recurrence r_(n+2) = r_(n+1) + r_n.

theorems.name: Book4A.goldenRatio_pos
theorems.statement: The golden spiral's growth coefficient is strictly positive.

theorems.name: Book4A.goldenSpiral_ratio_eq
theorems.statement: For nonzero initial radius, every consecutive golden-spiral magnitude ratio is exactly the golden ratio.

theorems.name: Book4A.goldenSpiral_ratio_tendsto
theorems.statement: The consecutive-radius ratios of a nondegenerate golden spiral converge to the golden ratio.

theorems.name: Book4A.contractionRefinement_iterate
theorems.statement: For a kappa-Lipschitz refinement operator with kappa<1, n-fold iteration contracts distances by at least kappa^n.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.contractingWith
theorems.statement: The Book 4 real-distance contraction axiom induces Mathlib's nonnegative contracting-map interface.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.ttprLimit_fixed
theorems.statement: On a nonempty complete metric symbolic space, the TTPR limit is fixed by one further refinement.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.fixed_eq_ttprLimit
theorems.statement: Every refinement-fixed symbolic state equals the unique TTPR limit.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.tendsto_iterate_ttprLimit
theorems.statement: Every iterated refinement orbit converges to the unique TTPR limit in a nonempty complete metric symbolic space.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.selfReference_dist_ttprLimit_le
theorems.statement: The recursive self-reference sequence's distance to the TTPR identity is bounded by kappa^n times its initial distortion.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.selfReference_fixed_iff_eq_ttprLimit
theorems.statement: A real symbolic state is refinement-fixed exactly when it equals the unique TTPR identity.

theorems.name: Book4A.ContractionRefinement.tendsto_selfReferenceIterate
theorems.statement: Every recursively iterated real self-reference sequence converges to the unique TTPR identity.

theorems.name: Book4A.imbalanced_drift_exceeds_reflect
theorems.statement: On a drift-imbalanced region (drift > theta*reflection, theta>1, reflection nonneg), drift strictly exceeds reflection outright.

theorems.name: Book4A.adjacent_rotate
theorems.statement: Rotating the twelve-position chromatic wheel by any fixed amount preserves adjacency.

theorems.name: Book4A.opposite_involutive
theorems.statement: Diametric opposition on the chromatic wheel is an involution.

theorems.name: Book4A.opposite_add
theorems.statement: Rotation and diametric opposition commute on the chromatic wheel.

theorems.name: Book4A.swapPerm_breaks_adjacency
theorems.statement: Countermodel: the non-rotation transposition swapping wheel positions 1 and 6 sends an adjacent pair (0,1) to a non-adjacent pair (0,6).

theorems.name: Book4B.cascadeProb_nonneg
theorems.statement: Fragmentation propagation probability is never negative

theorems.name: Book4B.cascadeProb_lt_one
theorems.statement: Fragmentation propagation probability never reaches certainty

theorems.name: Book4B.cascadeProb_strictMono
theorems.statement: Propagation probability strictly increases with the coupling integral

theorems.name: Book4B.repairSufficiency_decrease_accum
theorems.statement: Telescoped bound on a fragmentation measure that decreases by a fixed amount each repair step

theorems.name: Book4B.repairSufficiency_terminates
theorems.statement: A nonnegative, fixed-step-decreasing fragmentation measure cannot decrease past a computable step count

theorems.name: Book4B.recovery_threshold_exceeds_crit
theorems.statement: Reflective-reentry threshold transitivity: overlap meeting a super-critical recovery threshold exceeds the critical value

theorems.name: Book4B.coreCoherence_exceeds_crit
theorems.statement: Self-healing threshold transitivity: core coherence exceeding 1 minus a sub-critical core error exceeds 1 minus the critical error

theorems.name: Book4B.repairCapacityBound_nonneg
theorems.statement: Repair-capacity ceiling 1 - 1/(n_max+1) is never negative

theorems.name: Book4B.repairCapacityBound_lt_one
theorems.statement: Repair-capacity ceiling is always strictly below 1

theorems.name: Book4B.repairCapacityBound_strictMono
theorems.statement: Repair-capacity ceiling strictly rises with recursive depth capacity

theorems.name: Book4B.repairCapacity_lt_one
theorems.statement: Any repair capacity bounded by the ceiling is itself strictly below 1

theorems.name: Book4B.symbolicFreedom_of_flowFreedom
theorems.statement: A flow exhibiting flow freedom witnesses symbolic freedom (Freedom Criterion, forward direction)

theorems.name: Book4B.id_has_no_escape
theorems.statement: Countermodel: the identity flow can never supply flow freedom's escape clause

theorems.name: Book4B.autonomy_implies_freedom
theorems.statement: A decision/goal composite exhibiting flow freedom witnesses symbolic freedom

theorems.name: Book4B.freedomMeasure_nonneg
theorems.statement: Symbolic freedom measure (entropy ratio) is never negative

theorems.name: Book4B.freedomMeasure_le_one
theorems.statement: Symbolic freedom measure is at most 1

theorems.name: Book4B.constraintDomain_strict_growth
theorems.statement: A monotone constraint-domain sequence that ever changes is a strict superset of its start

theorems.name: Book4B.mem_constraintLimit_iff
theorems.statement: Membership in the recursive constraint limit is membership at some finite stage

theorems.name: Book4B.constraintDomain_subset_limit
theorems.statement: Every finite constraint domain embeds in the union-limit domain

theorems.name: Book4B.constraintLimit_least
theorems.statement: The recursive constraint limit is the least domain containing every finite stage

theorems.name: Book4B.constraintDirectedSystem_universal
theorems.statement: Monotone Book 4 constraint domains instantiate the Scholium directed system and uniquely mediate into their union-limit

theorems.name: Book4B.constraintLimit_tail_eq
theorems.statement: The union-limit of a monotone constraint sequence is invariant under discarding its first stage

theorems.name: Book4B.constraintLimit_strict_growth
theorems.statement: Any genuine finite constraint liberation makes the limit domain strictly extend the initial domain

theorems.name: Book4B.orbitConstraintDomain_mono
theorems.statement: Finite domains generated by a Scholium flow orbit form a monotone sequence

theorems.name: Book4B.orbitConstraintLimit_eq_range
theorems.statement: The Book 4 limit of finite Scholium flow domains is exactly the range of the full orbit

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteConstraint_leastFixedPoint_fixed
theorems.statement: Every monotone constraint map has a least fixed point by Knaster-Tarski

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteConstraint_leastFixedPoint_le
theorems.statement: The least constraint fixed point lies below every other fixed domain

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteConstraint_stage_le_fixed
theorems.statement: Ordinal limit induction proves every bottom-started stage lies below every fixed domain

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteConstraint_stabilized_fixed
theorems.statement: An eventually constant ordinal constraint iteration stabilizes at a genuine fixed point

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteConstraint_stabilized_eq_lfp
theorems.statement: A stabilized bottom-started ordinal iteration equals the least fixed point

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteIterate_exists_fixed_of_card_lt
theorems.statement: A sufficiently large well-ordered index forces an inflationary monotone constraint iteration to attain a fixed stage

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteIterate_le_fixed
theorems.statement: Every bottom-started standard transfinite iterate lies below every fixed domain

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteIterate_fixed_stage_eq_lfp
theorems.statement: Any fixed stage reached by the bottom-started traversal is the least fixed point

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteIterate_eventually_constant_of_fixed
theorems.statement: After an inflationary transfinite iteration reaches a fixed stage, every later stage is identical

theorems.name: Book4B.transfiniteIterate_eventually_constant_of_card_lt
theorems.statement: A cardinally large well-ordered traversal eventually becomes constant at its least fixed point

theorems.name: Book4B.freedomLife_succ_lt
theorems.statement: Freedom-life transition: freedom measure strictly rises at every step

theorems.name: Book4B.freedomLife_strictMono
theorems.statement: Freedom-life transition: freedom measure is strictly monotone over all steps

theorems.name: Book4B.freedomLife_increase_accum
theorems.statement: Telescoped lower bound on a freedom measure that rises by a fixed amount every step

theorems.name: Book4B.boundedErrorTerm_mono_linear
theorems.statement: Chain/Sum Rule error-scaling schema: a linear-in-epsilon bound is nondecreasing in the observer resolution

theorems.name: Book4B.boundedErrorTerm_mono_quadratic
theorems.statement: Product/Quotient/Power Rule error-scaling schema: a quadratic-in-epsilon bound is nondecreasing in the observer resolution

theorems.name: Book4B.quotientFloor_ge_eps_sq
theorems.statement: Fuzzy Quotient Rule's resolution floor is always at least epsilon squared

theorems.name: Book4B.fuzzyGradient_bound_antitone
theorems.statement: Fuzzy gradient's partial-derivative bound M_f/epsilon strictly falls as observer resolution grows

theorems.name: Book4B.gradientStability_same_resolution
theorems.statement: Gradient stability collapses to the quadratic correction alone when two observers share a resolution

theorems.name: Book9.cognitivelyFree_iff_internal_gradient
theorems.statement: Cognitive freedom means exactly that an internal governing drift exists and equals the system-generated gradient

theorems.name: Book9.cognitivelyFree_of_gradient_internal
theorems.statement: An internally generated gradient supplies the governing-drift witness for cognitive freedom

theorems.name: Book9.operatorReflexive_next
theorems.statement: An operator-reflexive evolution obeys its typed one-step internal modification recurrence

theorems.name: Book9.accountability_gap_lt_epsCrit
theorems.statement: An accountable system's identity gap 1-upsilon is strictly below its criticality threshold epsCrit.

theorems.name: Book9.accountability_gap_lt_epsMask
theorems.statement: If epsCrit <= epsMask, an accountable system's identity gap is also controlled by epsMask.

theorems.name: Book9.masking_not_accountable
theorems.statement: A masking event (divergence strictly above epsMask) is incompatible with the relational-viability clause of accountability against the same epsMask.

theorems.name: Book9.bidirectionalSRMF_fwd_injective
theorems.statement: In a bidirectional SRMF, the forward regulation map is injective.

theorems.name: Book9.bidirectionalSRMF_bwd_injective
theorems.statement: In a bidirectional SRMF, the backward regulation map is injective.

theorems.name: Book9.bidirectionalSRMF_fwd_bwd_fwd
theorems.statement: fwd . bwd . fwd collapses to fwd (symmetry-restoring reframing).

theorems.name: Book9.covenantDensity_regime_exhaustive
theorems.statement: Covenant drift density rho is always breach (<1), critical (=1), or cooperative (>1).

theorems.name: Book9.covenantDensity_regime_exclusive
theorems.statement: The breach/critical/cooperative regimes for covenant drift density are pairwise mutually exclusive.

theorems.name: Book9.freedomGrowing_not_global_min
theorems.statement: A configuration whose freedom is growing (some reachable state has strictly lower free energy) cannot already be a global free-energy minimizer.

theorems.name: Book9.freedomEntropyLaw_strict_max
theorems.statement: Under two-sided monotonicity around an equilibrium entropy tolerance, freedom at any other entropy value is strictly less than freedom at the equilibrium.

theorems.name: Book9.emergentAutonomy_min_exists
theorems.statement: A minimizer of symbolic free energy exists over any nonempty finite set of regulation parameters.

theorems.name: Book9.recursiveUpdate_eq_orbit
theorems.statement: The state at stage n of a stage-indexed update law L(n+1)=R n (L n) equals the n-fold fold of the per-stage updates from stage 0.

theorems.name: Book9.liberationDescent_Lambda_antitone
theorems.statement: The liberation potential Lambda is non-increasing along the recursive liberation dynamic.

theorems.name: Book9.liberationDescent_telescoped
theorems.statement: The total drift accumulated over k steps from stage n is bounded by the liberation potential's decrease over that range.

theorems.name: Book9.liberationDescent_converges
theorems.statement: A liberation descent trajectory converges in every complete metric state space

theorems.name: Book9.liberationDescent_converges_in_closed
theorems.statement: A liberation descent orbit contained in a closed basin converges to a limit in that basin

theorems.name: Book9.uniformOperatorLimit_fixedPoint
theorems.statement: The limit of a recursive orbit is fixed by its continuous uniform operator limit

theorems.name: Book9.limit_eq_of_zeroDescent_singleton
theorems.statement: A limiting zero-descent state is unique when the zero-descent set is a singleton

theorems.name: Book9.homeostaticLiberation_converges
theorems.statement: A Book 9 descent path of Book 6 closed power kernels converges in the complete finite function space

theorems.name: Book9.homeostaticLiberation_all
theorems.statement: Book 3 metabolic homeostasis persists at every finite stage of Book 6 closed-power Book 9 liberation

theorems.name: Book9.finalCollapseInversion
theorems.statement: If the realization map sends the limiting freedom operator to a frozen state, the collapse-inversion operator carries that frozen state on to the seed state.

theorems.name: Book9.symbolicFramework_iterate
theorems.statement: Every lawful transition Phi n of a symbolic framework is the n-fold iterate of the single-step generator Phi 1.

theorems.name: Book9.reinterpretation_dichotomy_exhaustive
theorems.statement: A re-interpretation's free-energy change deltaF is always either <=0 (Repair) or >0 (Distortion).

theorems.name: Book9.reinterpretation_dichotomy_exclusive
theorems.statement: The Repair (deltaF<=0) and Distortion (deltaF>0) modes of re-interpretation are mutually exclusive.

theorems.name: Book9.thermodynamicStress_nonneg
theorems.statement: Symbolic thermodynamic stress Sigma_AB is nonnegative whenever the dyad's joint free energy sits at or above its floor.

theorems.name: AppendixDH.phi_sq
theorems.statement: phi*phi = phi+1, the defining golden-ratio quadratic identity

theorems.name: AppendixDH.phi_gt_one
theorems.statement: 1 < phi

theorems.name: AppendixDH.phi_pos
theorems.statement: 0 < phi

theorems.name: AppendixDH.phi_fixed_point
theorems.statement: 1 + 1/phi = phi

theorems.name: AppendixDH.sustainable_phi
theorems.statement: phi is sustainable, with equality in the retention inequality

theorems.name: AppendixDH.sustainable_ge_phi
theorems.statement: phi is the least sustainable growth rate

theorems.name: AppendixDH.kappa_min_at_phi
theorems.statement: phi minimizes the entropy/curvature functional theta+1/theta among sustainable rates

theorems.name: AppendixDH.stability_phi_flow
theorems.statement: 1/(phi*phi) < 1, the flow-stability bound at phi

theorems.name: AppendixDH.fixed_point_iff_phi
theorems.statement: for lam>0, 1+1/lam=lam iff lam=phi (the unified fixed point)

theorems.name: AppendixDH.V_nonneg
theorems.statement: the symbolic potential V is nonnegative

theorems.name: AppendixDH.V_eq_zero_iff
theorems.statement: for C>0, V(C)=0 iff C=1

theorems.name: AppendixDH.Gop_phi_eigen
theorems.statement: (phi,1) is an eigenvector of the growth operator Gop with eigenvalue phi

theorems.name: AppendixDH.Gop_step
theorems.statement: Gop advances one step of any two-step-closure sequence

theorems.name: AppendixDH.tokensOfSet_eq_biUnion
theorems.statement: coarse-graining tokens equals the union of the individual token sets

theorems.name: AppendixDH.orthogonal_token_separation
theorems.statement: tokens resolving to distinct frame indices are disjoint

theorems.name: AppendixDH.mu_le_one
theorems.statement: the coherence budget never exceeds 1 on admissible subsets (PS-C1, derived)

theorems.name: AppendixDH.mu_biUnion_eq_sum
theorems.statement: binary finite additivity extends to any finite pairwise-disjoint indexed family

theorems.name: AppendixDH.orthogonal_additivity
theorems.statement: the coherence budget is additive over any finite mutually orthogonal family

theorems.name: AppendixDH.mu_conservation
theorems.statement: the budget sums to 1 across a fully-covering frame (PS-C3, derived)

theorems.name: AppendixDH.memoryAct_hist_changes
theorems.statement: every memory act strictly changes the history component

theorems.name: AppendixDH.memoryAct_irreversible
theorems.statement: every memory act incurs strictly positive metabolic cost

theorems.name: AppendixDH.memoryAct_order_iterate
theorems.statement: the history order parameter increases by at least n after n iterated acts

theorems.name: AppendixDH.memoryAct_no_return
theorems.statement: after n>=1 acts the history never returns to its earlier value

theorems.name: AppendixDH.dualHorizon_sufficiency
theorems.statement: m >= tauE/kappa forces deltaPhi >= tauE

theorems.name: AppendixDH.dualHorizon_necessity
theorems.statement: deltaPhi >= tauE forces m >= tauE/Lambda

theorems.name: AppendixDH.dualHorizon_necessity_pos
theorems.statement: deltaPhi >= tauE > 0 forces m > 0

theorems.name: AppendixDH.dualHorizon_tight_biconditional
theorems.statement: when kappa=Lambda, deltaPhi>=tauE iff m>=tauE/kappa

theorems.name: AppendixDH.dual_horizon_signature
theorems.statement: bounded reflexive emergence forces both G>0 and C>0 (Dual Horizon Necessity)

theorems.name: AppendixDH.geodesic_convergence
theorems.statement: distance to the stable region M shrinks geometrically under repeated contraction

theorems.name: ScholiumA.kernelBounded_le
theorems.statement: A drift bounded by δ, pushed through a norm-1 kernel via a submultiplicativity bound, stays bounded by δ

theorems.name: ScholiumA.ifValue_le_eps
theorems.statement: Under the c*eps factorization with c ≤ 1, the observed change never exceeds the resolution threshold eps (interpretability's boundedness half)

theorems.name: ScholiumA.nu_le_ifValue
theorems.statement: Under the c*eps factorization with c_min ≤ c and c_min*eps ≥ nu, the observed change is at least the distinguishability floor nu

theorems.name: ScholiumA.interpretable_of_factor_and_traceable
theorems.statement: The factorization sandwich plus an explicit finite traceability witness together give full (I1)-(I3) interpretability

theorems.name: ScholiumA.twoStep_bound
theorems.statement: A stage-composite operator (stabilize after drift), each step bounded by δ, approximates the identity to within 2δ by the triangle inequality

theorems.name: ScholiumA.chainedApprox_telescope
theorems.statement: Observer-metric distance across a stage range [m,n) is bounded by the telescoped sum of per-step bounds

theorems.name: ScholiumA.ChainedApprox.cauchySeq
theorems.statement: A Scholium stage path with summably decaying resolution steps is Cauchy.

theorems.name: ScholiumA.ChainedApprox.exists_limit_with_tail_bound
theorems.statement: In a complete observer metric, summable resolution decay yields a limiting stage with a tail-sum displacement bound.

theorems.name: Book4A.chainedApprox_yields_recursiveEncoding_limit
theorems.statement: Every complete-metric Scholium stage chain supplies a convergent Book 4 recursive identity encoding with the inherited tail bound.

theorems.name: ScholiumA.mirror_involution_ne_id_exists
theorems.statement: There exists an inner-product-preserving involution on Real×Real that is neither the identity nor pointwise negation

theorems.name: ScholiumA.projection_idempotent_ne_id_exists
theorems.statement: There exists an idempotent map on Real×Real that is not the identity

theorems.name: ScholiumA.stepZMod4_four_returns
theorems.statement: The successor map on ZMod 4, iterated four times, returns every point to itself

theorems.name: ScholiumA.stepZMod4_two_no_return
theorems.statement: The successor map on ZMod 4, iterated twice, does not return every point to itself

theorems.name: ScholiumA.contradictionIntensity_eq
theorems.statement: Contradiction intensity equals |1-λ|*|D_V| under exact opposition D_U = -λ*D_V

theorems.name: ScholiumA.contradictionIntensity_zero_of_lam_one
theorems.statement: When λ=1 (exact opposition), the two drifts cancel and contradiction intensity is zero

theorems.name: ScholiumA.separable_mixedDiff_zero
theorems.statement: Every additively separable (context-free) update has vanishing finite second difference

theorems.name: ScholiumA.nonseparable_of_mixedDiff_ne_zero
theorems.statement: A nonzero finite second difference certifies that an update is not additively separable, i.e. genuinely contextual

theorems.name: ScholiumA.quadratic_not_linear
theorems.statement: The quadratic coupling (x,y) ↦ x*y cannot be written as any linear coupling β0*x + β1*y

theorems.name: ScholiumA.linear_double
theorems.statement: A linear map L satisfies L(x+x) = 2*L(x), the stated algebraic premise behind 'reflexivity requires quadratic framing'

theorems.name: ScholiumA.fixedPointInheritance
theorems.statement: Conjugating any map g fixing x by an invertible map f gives a map fixing f(x); linearity of f is not needed

theorems.name: ScholiumB.reflexiveIterate_eq_iterate
theorems.statement: The reflect_n recursion of definition:bk1_reflexive_encoding_depth agrees with ordinary function iteration F^[n].

theorems.name: ScholiumB.reflexiveIterate_add
theorems.statement: Reflexive depths compose additively: reflect_{m+n} = reflect_m of reflect_n.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.no_irony_of_shallow_or_flat
theorems.statement: Contrapositive: depth < 2 or zero curvature implies an architecture cannot encode irony.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.no_irony_of_real_only
theorems.statement: Contrapositive: real-only (zero imaginative distance) architectures cannot encode irony.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.idempotent_fixes_image
theorems.statement: Every point in the image of an idempotent self-map is already a fixed point.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.idempotent_fixLocus_nonempty
theorems.statement: An idempotent self-map's fixed locus is nonempty whenever its image is nonempty.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.gibbsWeight_pos
theorems.statement: The finite Gibbs weight exp(-beta H(i)) is always strictly positive.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.gibbsZ_pos
theorems.statement: The finite partition function is strictly positive over a nonempty finite index type.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.gibbsProb_pos
theorems.statement: The finite Gibbs/equilibrium probability of every state is strictly positive.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.gibbsProb_sum_eq_one
theorems.statement: The finite Gibbs distribution is normalized: its probabilities sum to 1.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.gibbsProb_antitone
theorems.statement: At positive inverse temperature, lower-energy states receive no less equilibrium probability.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.minimalPS_collapse_ne_id
theorems.statement: The minimal linear PS-model's collapse P is not the identity map.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.minimalPS_drift_reflection_noncommute
theorems.statement: The minimal linear PS-model's drift and stabilization operators do not commute.

theorems.name: ScholiumB.minimalPS_connection_curvature_nonzero
theorems.statement: The minimal linear PS-model's symbolic connection has nonzero curvature.

theorems.name: SmallPack.resolutionLevel_mono
theorems.statement: Nested resolution levels of the symbolic tower are monotone: satisfying the complexity/operator-norm bounds at level lambda implies satisfying them at any coarser level mu >= lambda.

theorems.name: SmallPack.symbolicEnergy_nonneg
theorems.statement: The symbolic energy functional H + (1/2)*drift^2 + (epsO/2)*refl^2 is nonnegative whenever the Hamiltonian term and observer-reflection weight are nonnegative.

theorems.name: SmallPack.symbolicEnergyContraction_accum
theorems.statement: Telescoped form of the energy contraction law: the total drift-plus-reflection cost accumulated over n steps is bounded by the total energy drop from step 0 to step n.

theorems.name: SmallPack.symbolicEnergyContraction_sum_bounded
theorems.statement: If the energy sequence never drops below a floor L, the cumulative squared-drift-plus-reflection cost over any number of steps is uniformly bounded, independent of n.

theorems.name: SmallPack.symbolicEnergyContraction_term_bounded
theorems.statement: Consequence of the cumulative bound: each individual step's squared drift is uniformly bounded across all steps -- the honest per-step substitute for 'SRV trajectories are Cauchy.'

theorems.name: SmallPack.chartBound_mono
theorems.statement: The chart-bound expression C_chart * sqrt(lambda) is monotone nondecreasing in the resolution level lambda.

theorems.name: SmallPack.chartBound_nonneg
theorems.statement: The chart-bound expression C_chart * sqrt(lambda) is always nonnegative.

theorems.name: SmallPack.inf_mono_of_subset
theorems.statement: Enlarging a feasible real-valued constraint set can only decrease, or leave unchanged, the least-norm satisfying cost (monotonicity of sInf under subset).

theorems.name: SmallPack.inf_strict_decrease
theorems.statement: The decrease is strict exactly when the enlarged feasible set contains a witness strictly below the old infimum, i.e. the new parameter direction is non-redundant.

theorems.name: Asymptotics.Contraction.iterate_dist_le
theorems.statement: after n iterations of a contraction, distance to the fixed point is bounded by rate^n times the initial distance

theorems.name: Asymptotics.Contraction.tendsto_fixedPt
theorems.statement: the iterate sequence of a contraction converges to its fixed point

theorems.name: Asymptotics.GeometricErrorBound.tendsto_zero
theorems.statement: an error sequence dominated by C*rate^n (rate in [0,1)) tends to zero

theorems.name: Asymptotics.QuadraticErrorBound.tendsto_zero
theorems.statement: an error sequence dominated by C*eps_n^2 with eps_n -> 0 tends to zero

theorems.name: Asymptotics.windowAverage_mem_unitInterval
theorems.statement: a windowed average of a {0,1}-valued bifurcation indicator always lies in [0,1]

theorems.name: Asymptotics.MutationEquilibrium.eventually_pos
theorems.statement: at mutation equilibrium the mutation rate is eventually strictly positive

theorems.name: Asymptotics.MutabilityEquilibrium.eta_tendsto_of_mu_tendsto
theorems.statement: if mutation rate and mutation-minus-damping both converge, reflective damping converges to the same limit as the mutation rate

theorems.name: Asymptotics.AntitoneBoundedProcess.tendsto_iInf
theorems.statement: an antitone process bounded below converges to its infimum

theorems.name: Asymptotics.eventually_mem_ball_of_tendsto
theorems.statement: a sequence converging to a point is eventually inside every epsilon-ball around that point

theorems.name: Asymptotics.AsymptoticExponentField.eventually_near_one
theorems.statement: an observer exponent tending to 1 as horizon grows is eventually within any delta>0 of 1 from above

theorems.name: Book2H.dataProcessing_kl
theorems.statement: TIER 3 -- the data-processing inequality: relative entropy KL(evolve P rho || evolve P sigma) <= KL(rho || sigma) for any row-stochastic kernel P, density rho, and reference sigma with sigma>0 and evolve P sigma > 0 columnwise; proved by applying the log-sum inequality column by column and summing with the stochastic row-sum identity.

theorems.name: Book2H.h_theorem
theorems.statement: TIER 4 -- THE DISCRETE H-THEOREM (theorem:bk2_h_theorem_for_symbolic_evol, dynamical half): for P row-stochastic, beta>0, and P in detailed balance with gibbs(beta,H), one evolution step does not increase the free energy: freeEnergy beta H (evolve P rho) <= freeEnergy beta H rho for every density rho. Derived from freeEnergy_eq_kl (Book2.lean) plus dataProcessing_kl specialized to sigma = gibbs beta H, using detailedBalance_stationary to fix the reference measure under P and gibbs_pos for its columnwise positivity.

theorems.name: Book2H.trajectory_isDensity
theorems.statement: Every state along a repeated row-stochastic finite trajectory remains a probability density.

theorems.name: Book2H.freeEnergy_trajectory_antitone
theorems.statement: FINITE GRADIENT-FLOW KERNEL (theorem:bk2_wasserstein_gradient_flow): repeated detailed-balance evolution generates an antitone free-energy trajectory; no continuum Wasserstein metric or Fokker-Planck identification is asserted.

theorems.name: Book2Response.fluctuation_response_hasDerivAt
theorems.statement: FINITE STATIC KUBO IDENTITY (theorem:bk2_symbolic_fluctuation_dissipation_relation): under H_h=H-hB, the derivative at h=0 of the Gibbs expectation of A equals beta times the equilibrium covariance of A and B; no continuous-time semigroup is asserted.

theorems.name: Book2Consistency.thermodynamic_consistency
theorems.statement: Conditional hypothesis-surface balance: explicit first variation plus closed observer-energy/temperature-entropy cancellation yields free-energy work equal to minus entropy-temperature exchange.

theorems.name: Book2Consistency.boundedCurvature_alone_insufficient
theorems.statement: Countermodel: a strict curvature bound alone does not force the hypothesis-surface thermodynamic balance; the source proof's closed-balance premise is load-bearing.

theorems.name: Book5Adaptation.adapted_state_viable
theorems.statement: Adaptation as explicit data: under a condition change, a selected transfer, reflection, or drift update preserves viability.

theorems.name: Book5Adaptation.operators_do_not_supply_adaptation
theorems.statement: Countermodel: possessing all three named update channels does not itself guarantee a viability-preserving adaptation.

theorems.name: Book5ConvergenceMAP.mapShare_zero
theorems.statement: The explicit MAP population-share trajectory begins at its supplied initial share.

theorems.name: Book5ConvergenceMAP.mapShare_succ
theorems.statement: Each replicator step retains a fixed fraction of the remaining non-MAP population.

theorems.name: Book5ConvergenceMAP.mapShare_tendsto_one
theorems.statement: A contraction factor in the interval from zero through one converges the MAP population share to one.

theorems.name: Book5ConvergenceMAP.increasing_drift_alone_does_not_force_map_convergence
theorems.statement: Countermodel: strictly increasing drift can coexist with a population share fixed at one half.

theorems.name: Book5ESSEquivalence.discreteSetDistance_eq_zero_iff
theorems.statement: The finite discrete strategy-set distance vanishes exactly when the two strategy sets are equal.

theorems.name: AppendixTitansArrow.memorization_changes_history
theorems.statement: A witnessed test-time memory act strictly changes its history component.

theorems.name: AppendixTitansArrow.memorization_has_positive_cost
theorems.statement: The Appendix C memory witness supplies positive computational cost.

theorems.name: AppendixTitansArrow.titans_arrow_of_time
theorems.statement: Positive iterations of a witnessed memory process cannot return to their original history.

theorems.name: AppendixTitansArrow.visible_return_is_not_full_return
theorems.statement: Visible model-state return can coexist with irreversible history advance.

theorems.name: AppendixTitansArrow.bare_testTime_update_need_not_be_irreversible
theorems.statement: Countermodel: a bare test-time update can be exactly reversible without a memory-law witness.

theorems.name: Book5ESSEquivalence.CriticalIdentification.sets_eq
theorems.statement: Two-sided critical ESS/MAP identification proves equality of the strategy sets.

theorems.name: Book5ESSEquivalence.CriticalIdentification.distance_zero
theorems.statement: Critical ESS/MAP identification forces zero discrete strategy-set distance.

theorems.name: Book5ESSEquivalence.distance_tendsto_zero_of_eventually_identified
theorems.statement: Eventual two-sided ESS/MAP identification makes the discrete strategy-set distance converge to zero.

theorems.name: Book5ESSEquivalence.population_concentration_alone_does_not_identify_strategy_sets
theorems.statement: Countermodel: population concentration on MAP does not alone identify the abstract ESS and MAP sets.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorSelection.exists_process_minimizer
theorems.statement: Every nonempty finite operator inventory contains a process-free-energy minimizer.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorSelection.selected_le_incumbent
theorems.statement: A certified SRMF selection costs no more process free energy than any available incumbent.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorSelection.rejects_strictly_suboptimal_incumbent
theorems.statement: A certified minimizer rejects an incumbent when a strictly cheaper available operator exists.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorSelection.viability_alone_does_not_force_operator_argmin
theorems.statement: Countermodel: viability alone does not force selection of the process-free-energy argmin.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorAdaptation.refinementVelocity_eq_of_below
theorems.statement: Below threshold, explicit refinement velocity is proportional to the effectiveness shortfall.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorAdaptation.refinementVelocity_pos
theorems.statement: Positive feedback gain gives a strictly positive refinement rate below threshold.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorAdaptation.gradientStep_displacement
theorems.statement: The operator displacement is the negative process-free-energy gradient scaled by its step size.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorAdaptation.quadratic_processFreeEnergy_descent
theorems.statement: A stable gradient step descends the quadratic process-free-energy kernel.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorAdaptation.process_descent_can_increase_execution_cost
theorems.statement: Process-free-energy descent can coexist with a transient increase in execution cost.

theorems.name: Book5OperatorAdaptation.below_threshold_alone_does_not_force_adaptation
theorems.statement: Countermodel: below-threshold effectiveness alone does not force an operator update.

theorems.name: Book5ReflectiveAccuracy.fidelity_le_log_of_depth_bound
theorems.statement: A bounded per-level fidelity gain composed with a logarithmic metabolic depth bound yields logarithmically bounded reflective accuracy.

theorems.name: Book5ReflectiveAccuracy.fidelityEnvelope_nonneg
theorems.statement: The logarithmic fidelity envelope is nonnegative at nonnegative gain and metabolic capacity.

theorems.name: Book5ReflectiveAccuracy.capacity_alone_does_not_bound_unconstrained_fidelity
theorems.statement: Countermodel: metabolic capacity alone cannot bound an otherwise unconstrained fidelity value.

theorems.name: Book5ShadeTransfer.shade_preserved_of_radius_preserved
theorems.statement: Exact radius preservation transports every shade normalization unchanged.

theorems.name: Book5ShadeTransfer.radial_order_alone_does_not_preserve_shade
theorems.statement: Countermodel: strict radial-order preservation need not preserve radial or shade values.

theorems.name: Book5ShadeTransfer.golden_logRadius_step
theorems.statement: Every golden-spiral transition increases log-radius by exactly log phi.

theorems.name: Book5ShadeTransfer.normalized_shade_is_not_multiplicative
theorems.statement: Countermodel: multiplicative radius steps do not remain multiplicative under bounded shade normalization.

theorems.name: Book5ShadeTransfer.balanced_reciprocity_paints_golden_rate
theorems.statement: Balanced reciprocity has the golden-ratio spectral growth rate.

theorems.name: Book5ShadeTransfer.extraction_has_unit_radial_rate
theorems.statement: Zero reciprocal weight has unit spectral rate and hence no radial multiplication.

theorems.name: Book6DriftMutation.mutationRate_eq_weighted_curvature_change
theorems.statement: Mutation rate is the density-weighted magnitude of curvature change along drift.

theorems.name: Book6DriftMutation.mutationRate_nonneg
theorems.statement: Nonnegative symbolic density gives nonnegative global mutation rate.

theorems.name: Book6DriftMutation.mutationRate_le_uniform_curvature_bound
theorems.statement: A normalized density transports a uniform directional-curvature bound to the global mutation rate.

theorems.name: Book6DriftMutation.drift_alone_does_not_determine_mutation_rate
theorems.statement: Countermodel: the same density and drift label permit distinct mutation rates when curvature response differs.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicMutation.mem_feasibleStates_iff
theorems.statement: Reflective entropy thresholding exactly characterizes the finite feasible mutation states.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicMutation.exists_constrained_mepp
theorems.statement: Every nonempty finite reflectively feasible population has a maximum-entropy-production state.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicMutation.equilibrium_balance_alone_does_not_imply_mepp
theorems.statement: Countermodel: equality of production and dissipation at equilibrium does not imply constrained maximality.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceGradient.confidenceDrivenVelocity_eq
theorems.statement: The scalar confidence-driven mutation velocity decomposes into drift, diffusion, and fluctuation terms.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceGradient.pure_confidence_drift_descends
theorems.statement: Without diffusion or noise, nonnegative drift coefficient moves non-increasingly along confidence.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceGradient.pure_confidence_drift_strict
theorems.statement: Positive pure confidence drift is strictly descending away from confidence-critical points.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceGradient.diffusion_can_reverse_confidence_drift
theorems.statement: Counterexample: diffusion can reverse the direction of the confidence drift term.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceGradient.regularity_alone_does_not_force_confidence_dynamics
theorems.statement: Countermodel: regular confidence data alone does not impose the printed mutation velocity law.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.fixed_temperature_freeEnergy_increment
theorems.statement: At fixed temperature, the exact free-energy increment is dE minus T times dS.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.fixed_temperature_firstLaw_reduction
theorems.statement: Substituting the ordinary energy balance cancels the entropy term from the fixed-temperature free-energy increment.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.varying_temperature_freeEnergy_increment
theorems.statement: Changing temperature adds the exact temperature-entropy and finite cross terms to the free-energy increment.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.printed_firstLaw_not_implied_by_freeEnergy_definition
theorems.statement: Counterexample: the printed positive entropy term conflicts with F equals E minus T times S at fixed energy and temperature.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.oriented_freeEnergy_fixed_temperature_increment
theorems.statement: An explicit potential orientation determines the entropy sign in its fixed-temperature increment.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.printed_and_derived_laws_agree_iff_entropy_static
theorems.statement: At nonzero temperature, the printed and derived candidate laws agree exactly when entropy is static.

theorems.name: Book6ThermodynamicConsistency.interface_term_reconciles_printed_law_iff
theorems.statement: An interface reconciles the candidate laws exactly when it uniquely supplies T times dS.

theorems.name: Book4ImaginationGuard.phaseBudget_append
theorems.statement: Concatenated traversal witnesses add phase exposure without erasing intermediate cancellation.

theorems.name: Book4ImaginationGuard.effectiveRate_lt_one_iff_phase_penalty_below_margin
theorems.statement: Phase-aware refinement remains contractive exactly while its penalty stays below unused contraction margin.

theorems.name: Book4ImaginationGuard.eleven_percent_phase_ends_near_boundary_contraction
theorems.statement: Eleven-percent phase exposure ends strict contraction when the base rate is at least eighty-nine percent.

theorems.name: Book4ImaginationGuard.projection_equality_can_hide_unsafe_phase
theorems.statement: Countermodel: identical observable projections can hide distinct phase safety and contraction status.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceStability.stabilityVelocity_eq
theorems.statement: The scalar confidence-stability velocity is negative coupling times the Hamiltonian quotient slope.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceStability.stabilityVelocity_nonpos_of_quotientSlope_nonneg
theorems.statement: Nonnegative coupling and quotient slope give nonpositive stability velocity.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceStability.constant_hamiltonian_gives_positive_stabilityVelocity
theorems.statement: A positive constant confidence Hamiltonian makes the printed outer-minus coupling increase stability.

theorems.name: Book6ConfidenceStability.values_alone_do_not_force_confidence_stability_coupling
theorems.statement: Countermodel: confidence and stability values alone do not determine their velocity law.

theorems.name: Book6ObserverExtension.observerExtension_agrees
theorems.statement: An observer-local field extension agrees exactly on its admitted domain.

theorems.name: Book6ObserverExtension.exists_observerExtension
theorems.statement: Every observer-local field has a total extension once its outside-domain fallback is supplied.

theorems.name: Book6ObserverExtension.observer_error_accumulates
theorems.statement: Approximation errors add across consecutive observer and interface transports.

theorems.name: Book6ObserverExtension.observer_bound_alone_does_not_force_identity_preservation
theorems.statement: Countermodel: a curvature bound alone does not preserve an arbitrary divergence or entropy identity.

theorems.name: Book8OrientationSignposting.next_four
theorems.statement: The canonical Observe-Project-Reflect-Update cognition cycle returns after exactly four directed stages.

theorems.name: Book8OrientationSignposting.transport_preserves_canonical_change
theorems.statement: Explicit audience-frame transport preserves the canonical change while adapting its displayed sign.

theorems.name: Book8OrientationSignposting.opposite_signs_agree_iff_zero
theorems.statement: Aligned and reversed audience signs agree exactly when the represented change is zero.

theorems.name: Book8OrientationSignposting.reversed_display_of_positive
theorems.statement: A positive canonical change displays negatively in a reversed audience frame.

theorems.name: Book8OrientationSignposting.displayedNext_differs_across_orientations
theorems.statement: Opposite presentation orientations display different next arrows without redefining the canonical source order.

theorems.name: Book7SystemicPower.localPower_pos
theorems.statement: Positive confidence, gradient magnitude, and effective volume yield positive local symbolic power.

theorems.name: Book7SystemicPower.systemicPower_pos
theorems.statement: A nonempty finite basin with positive density and local factors has strictly positive systemic power.

theorems.name: Book7SystemicPower.high_confidence_alone_does_not_force_power
theorems.statement: Countermodel: maximal confidence produces no power on a flat confidence landscape.

theorems.name: Book7SystemicPower.gradient_reversal_preserves_unoriented_power
theorems.statement: The norm-valued local-power integrand is invariant under reversal of gradient orientation.

theorems.name: Book7SystemicPower.equal_power_does_not_determine_gradient_orientation
theorems.statement: Countermodel: opposite directed gradients have identical positive scalar power.

theorems.name: Book8CognitiveScaffold.ComposablePair.step_closed
theorems.statement: Metabolize-then-debug preserves the explicitly admitted operational domain.

theorems.name: Book8CognitiveScaffold.ComposablePair.iterate_closed
theorems.statement: Every finite iterate of a composable closed pair remains admissible.

theorems.name: momentum_residual_tendsto_zero

theorems.name: Book4Fz.TopologicalObserverTangentAtlas.coordinateOverlap_isOpen

theorems.name: Book4Fz.controlledVectorFieldPerturbation_apply

theorems.name: Book4Fz.controlledVectorFieldPerturbation_eq_self_iff
theorems.statement: At nonzero amplitude, no observed pointwise change is equivalent to a zero perturbation direction.

theorems.name: Book4Fz.IsObserverFlow.zero_eq_id

theorems.name: Book4Fz.IsObserverFlow.bijective

theorems.name: Book4Fz.CrossObserverTimeConsistent.reparam_zero

theorems.name: Book4Fz.CrossObserverTimeConsistent.reparam_add

theorems.name: Book4Fz.observerFlow_perturbation_iff

theorems.name: Book4D.contextualStructuralGrowth_induces_curvature
